pages.title: About Vicky - Vicky Foxcroft
pages.text_excerpt: About Vicky - Vicky Foxcroft Skip to main content Vicky Foxcroft Labour MP for Lewisham North About Vicky Contact & Surgeries In Lewisham North In Parliament Latest Contributions My Time as Shadow Minister for Disabled People Disability Confident Roundtable 04/03/24 Reasonable Adjustments Roundtable – 14/09/23 Pathways for Young Disabled People Roundtable – 16/05/23 Regional Roadshows Roadshow Write Ups Submission of Evidence #ShieldingStories Your Campaigns Animal Welfare Business, Science & Trade Culture, Media & Sport Defence Education Environment, Food & Farming Finance Health Home Affairs Housing International Affairs Transport Women & Equalities Work & Pensions Visit Parliament Youth Violence Commission FAQs Your Privacy More Search Search the website Search Menu About Vicky I was first elected to represent Lewisham North (previously named Lewisham Deptford) in 2015 and was delighted to be re-elected in 2017 with an increased majority of 34,899 (63.3% of the vote). I have lived in Lewisham North for well over 15 years now and it’s an absolute pleasure to represent the people and places I have got to know and love in that time. Prior to my election, I served as a local councillor in Brockley ward (2010-14) and was chair of the Lewisham Council Labour group. During those years the coalition government made vicious cuts to local government, but I helped to ensure that Lewisham Council still delivered on its promise to build council houses for the first time in a generation and continue to be a living wage employer. These are both issues I believe are central to tackling the cost of living crisis. I also worked for a trade union for 13 years and during my time there led campaigns against low pay, the exploitation of agency workers and the use of zero hour contracts. Again, these are issues I continue to feel strongly about and strive to promote in Parliament. I want to see Lewisham become a place where everyone receives a genuine living wage. Since I was elected to Parliament I have worked hard to be a strong voice for Lewisham North in Westminster. My focuses have been influenced by the issues encountered locally and by campaigns in the community. Perhaps most notably, following the deaths of five young people from the constituency shortly after I was elected and the rise of knife crime in the area, I arranged for a parliamentary debate on youth violence to take place. That debate called for the establishment of the Youth Violence Commission , which I now chair. Following his election as Leader in September 2015, Jeremy Corbyn appointed me as one of his Opposition Whips. The whips are responsible for looking after the well-being of our MPs, as well as organising for votes. In July 2019 I moved from the Whips Office to the Shadow Digital, Culture, Media and Sport team and became Shadow Minister for Civil Society. My role involved working closely with charities, volunteers and social enterprise organisations. In April 2020 our new Leader, Keir Starmer, moved me across to the Shadow Work and Pensions team where I became Shadow Minister for Disabled People. In July 2024, I was re-elected to serve as MP for Lewisham North with 57.7% of the vote and was appointed by the Prime Minister to be a Government Whip. I held this position until 19th June 2025, when I resigned over the Government’s proposed changes to disability and long-term sickness benefits . In October 2025 I achieved a long-held ambition of mine when I was elected to the Culture, Media & Sport Select Committee. I have always been hugely proud to represent such a creative constituency and this has given me a brilliant opportunity to do more work in this area. Thank you for taking the time to visit my website. I hope you have found it useful, but if there is anything else you would like to know or if I can be of help in any way, please feel free to contact my office . Vicky Foxcroft MP Lewisham North The constituency of Lewisham North stretches from the northernmost tip

pages.title: Contact & Surgeries - Vicky Foxcroft
headings: h3: Cost of Living Crisis: Constituent Support
pages.text_excerpt: Contact & Surgeries - Vicky Foxcroft Skip to main content Vicky Foxcroft Labour MP for Lewisham North About Vicky Contact & Surgeries In Lewisham North In Parliament Latest Contributions My Time as Shadow Minister for Disabled People Disability Confident Roundtable 04/03/24 Reasonable Adjustments Roundtable – 14/09/23 Pathways for Young Disabled People Roundtable – 16/05/23 Regional Roadshows Roadshow Write Ups Submission of Evidence #ShieldingStories Your Campaigns Animal Welfare Business, Science & Trade Culture, Media & Sport Defence Education Environment, Food & Farming Finance Health Home Affairs Housing International Affairs Transport Women & Equalities Work & Pensions Visit Parliament Youth Violence Commission FAQs Your Privacy More Search Search the website Search Menu Contact & Surgeries How to Contact Me Email: vicky.foxcroft.mp@parliament.uk Tel: 020 8469 4638 (11.00am-3.00pm Monday, Wednesday and Friday, excluding bank holidays. Please leave a message at other times.) Post: Vicky Foxcroft MP, House of Commons, London, SW1A 0AA If you contact me in writing, please remember to include your full name, address and telephone number (where appropriate). If you are contacting me about a casework matter, please also include any relevant reference numbers. These details will help us to deal with your case more efficiently. A list of local and national coronavirus information and support services can be found here and here. Advice Surgeries Please see below for the dates of Vicky’s forthcoming advice surgeries. If you’d like to attend, please get in touch with the office on 020 8469 4638 for an appointment. Friday 15th May 2026 – 3pm to 6pm Friday 5th June 2026 – 3pm to 6pm Friday 19th June 2026 – 3pm to 6pm Friday 3rd July 2026 – 3pm to 6pm Friday 17th July 2026 – 3pm to 6pm Cost of Living Crisis: Constituent Support If you are struggling with energy bills or other rising costs as a result of the cost of living crisis, you can find further guidance and information about support services in the constituency here. Urgent Assistance If you are in need of urgent assistance (especially when the office is closed), the following organisations may be of use to you: Samaritans – call: 116 123, text: 07725 90 90 90 or email jo@samaritans.org Further advice if you or someone you know needs mental health support: https://lewisham.gov.uk/myservices/socialcare/health/mental-health/get-help-with-mental-health Childline – counselling and advice for under 19s. 0800 1111 www.childline.org.uk National Domestic Violence Helpline – 0808 2000 247 www.refuge.org.uk Other DV support services are listed here: https://www.vickyfoxcroft.org.uk/uncategorised/2020/04/08/domestic-violence-support/ To make a non-urgent crime report – 101 Report ASB to LBL – https://lewisham.gov.uk/inmyarea/publicsafety/antisocial-behaviour/report-antisocial-behaviour or www.lewisham.gov.uk/cleanerlewisham . Lewisham Homes out-of-hours repairs team – 0800 028 2028 Lewisham Refugee and Migrant Network – 020 8694 0323 www.lrmn.org.uk Advice Lewisham – www.citizensadvicelewisham.org.uk British Gas emergency number – 0800 111 999 Thames Water emergency number – 0800 316 9800 If you have become homeless: Streetlink (and No Second Night Out) on 0300 500 0914 or https://www.streetlink.london/Streetlink_London_HomePage 999 Club – 0208 694 5797 Shelter – 0808 800 4444 All personal information given to me or my staff will be treated confidentially. In order to make representations on your behalf, we may need to pass your information to other agencies. If you have any queries regarding your personal data, please contact my office. I undertake to handle the information you supply in accordance with the Data Protection Act 1988 and the UK General Data Protection Regulation. You can read my privacy and data retention policy in full here . Disability Confident employer logo Follow on social media Line drawing of Deptford Vicky Foxcroft Contact details Email: vicky.foxcroft.mp@parliament

pages.title: Submission of Evidence - Vicky Foxcroft
headings: h3: New Deal for Disabled People
pages.text_excerpt: Submission of Evidence - Vicky Foxcroft Skip to main content Vicky Foxcroft Labour MP for Lewisham North About Vicky Contact & Surgeries In Lewisham North In Parliament Latest Contributions My Time as Shadow Minister for Disabled People Disability Confident Roundtable 04/03/24 Reasonable Adjustments Roundtable – 14/09/23 Pathways for Young Disabled People Roundtable – 16/05/23 Regional Roadshows Roadshow Write Ups Submission of Evidence #ShieldingStories Your Campaigns Animal Welfare Business, Science & Trade Culture, Media & Sport Defence Education Environment, Food & Farming Finance Health Home Affairs Housing International Affairs Transport Women & Equalities Work & Pensions Visit Parliament Youth Violence Commission FAQs Your Privacy More Search Search the website Search Menu Submission of Evidence I am seeking research and ideas which can feed into our consultation and help to inform my work as Shadow Minister for Disabled People. I plan to publish approved material on this page. If you have any work or evidence you think would be of interest, please upload it for review using the form below. To ensure ours files are accessible to as many people as possible, please upload your contributions as plain text documents instead of/in addition to PDF files. We will not share any personal details without your permission. You can view my full privacy policy here . Many thanks. Research Response to the National Disability Strategy (Disability At Work) – Kim Hoque, Warwick Business School, University of Warwick and Nick Bacon, Bayes Business School, City, University of London Work is Neighbourhood Business – Caroline Richardson & Simon Duffy An Introduction to Basic Income Plus – Caroline Richardson & Simon Duffy An Introduction to Basic Income Plus – Easy Read Version – Caroline Richardson & Simon Duffy Getting Disability Benefits Right the First Time – Scope Beyond the Barriers – Spartacus Network The Adoption of Preventable Harm, Masquerading as Social Policy Reforms (Journal of (In)Justice International) – Mo Stewart, Research Lead, Preventable Harm Project The Public Health Crisis Created by UK Social Policy Reforms (Justice, Power & Resistance) – Mo Stewart, Research Lead, Preventable Harm Project Social Policy Abused: The Creation of Preventable Harm (Social Policy Brief) – Mo Stewart, Research Lead, Preventable Harm Project New Deal for Disabled People Name First Last Email Phone Website File Max. file size: 10 MB. Consent I agree to the privacy policy.
